description: The root cause is a bug in `LiftoffAssembler::DropValue()` in `src/wasm/baseline/liftoff-assembler.cc`. When a WebAssembly exception value is dropped from the Liftoff baseline compiler's virtual stack state during `try/catch` handling, the old implementation simply used `std::copy` to shift the remaining `VarState` slots down by one position without recalculating their spill offsets. This breaks the invariant that stack slot offsets must be contiguous and properly aligned. In particular, alignment-requiring types such as `kS128` (SIMD 128-bit, needing 16-byte alignment) that follow the dropped exception slot retain their original (now incorrect) offsets instead of being recomputed via `NextSpillOffset`. The mismatch is detected by `ValidateCacheState()` as `DCHECK_EQ(offset, var.offset())` failing (e.g., computed offset 128 vs stored offset 112). In a release build (where DCHECKs are disabled), the Liftoff-compiled WebAssembly code would use the incorrect stack offsets, causing multiple stack slots to overlap in memory, potentially enabling stack memory corruption. The fix renames `DropValue` to `DropExceptionValueAtOffset` and adds logic to recompute and compact the offsets of all remaining slots (and physically move stack-resident values if necessary) after the exception slot is removed. This bug is triggered by compiling a WebAssembly module with `try/catch` blocks, where the catch state is not reached, causing the implicit exception reference slot to be dropped while aligned value types (e.g., `v128`) remain on the virtual stack above it.

fixes.evidence_text: [https://crrev.com/c/5596273](<https://crrev.com/c/5596273>) approved for merge; please merge to branch 12.6 at your earliest convenience / by EOD tomorrow, Thursday, 13 June so this fix can be included in the next M126 Stable update -- thanks!
